title: Mildly Garlicky Uruguayan Cheonggukjang Empanadas
description: A tender and energizing Uruguayan dish featuring cheonggukjang in a flaky crust, mildly garlicky and full of flavor, perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the garlic and onion until softened.
Add the cheonggukjang to the pan and stir to combine with the garlic and onion mixture.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the cheonggukjang mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the egg wash and fold the dough in half to form a half-moon shape.
Press the edges together to seal the empanada and use a fork to crimp the edges.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the egg wash and sprinkle with salt and black pepper.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
